name: footballmodels
documentation:
# footballmodels

Library for football models

classes.name: footballmodels.player_similarity.models.TemplateSimilarityAlgorithm
documentation:
Finds similar players based on features specified by
positional tempate

functions.name: footballmodels.opta.distance.progressive_distance
documentation:
Returns a boolean series indicating whether each event in a dataframe is a progressive pass

Args:
    whoscored_df (pd.DataFrame): The dataframe

Returns:
    pd.Series: True if the event is a progressive pass, False otherwise

classes.name: footballmodels.opta.distance.Distance

methods.name: footballmodels.opta.distance.Distance.__call__
documentation:
Computes the distance between two points on a football pitch

Args:
    x0 (np.ndarray): The x coordinates of the first points
    y0 (np.ndarray): The y coordinates of the first points
    x1 (np.ndarray): The x coordinates of the second points
    y1 (np.ndarray): The y coordinates of the second points
Computes actual distance between two points on a football pitch

functions.name: footballmodels.opta.passes.is_open_play_pass
documentation:
Returns a boolean series indicating whether each event in a dataframe is an open play pass

Args:
    data (pd.DataFrame): The dataframe

Returns:
    pd.Series: True if the event is an open play pass, False otherwise

functions.name: footballmodels.opta.functions.col_get_qualifier_value
documentation:
Get a value of a specific qualifier from an opta dataframe.
Dataframe must contain a column called 'qualifiers' which is a list of dict of form
{
    "type": {"value": int, "displayName": str},
    "value": int
}

Args:
    dataframe (pd.DataFrame): Dataframe containing a column called 'qualifiers'
    display_name (str, optional): The display name of the qualifier. Defaults to "".
    qualifier_code (int, optional): The qualifier code. Defaults to -1.

Returns:
    pd.Series: A series of the qualifier values

functions.name: footballmodels.opta.functions.has_qualifier
documentation:
Checks if a given qualifier is present in a dict of qualifiers

Args:
    qs (Dict[str, Any]): The qualifiers dict
    display_name (str): The display name of the qualifier
    qualifier_code (int): The code of the qualifier

Returns:
    bool: True if the qualifier is present, False otherwise

functions.name: footballmodels.opta.functions.col_has_qualifier
documentation:
Checks if a given qualifier is present in each element of a  column of a dataframe

Args:
    df (pd.DataFrame): The dataframe
    display_name (str): The display name of the qualifier
    qualifier_code (int): The code of the qualifier

Returns:
    pd.Series: True if the qualifier is present, False otherwise

functions.name: footballmodels.utils.distance.cartesian_distance
documentation:
Calculate the cartesian distance between a point and all other points in a set.

Args:
    point (np.ndarray): The point to calculate the distance from.
    all_points (np.ndarray): The set of points to calculate the distance to.

Returns:
    np.ndarray: The cartesian distance between the point and all other points in the set.

functions.name: footballmodels.utils.distance.weighted_cartesian_distance
documentation:
Calculate the weighted cartesian distance between a point and all other points in a set.

Args:
    point (np.ndarray): The point to calculate the distance from.
    all_points (np.ndarray): The set of points to calculate the distance to.
    weights (np.ndarray): The weights to apply to each dimension of the points.

Returns:
    np.ndarray: The weighted cartesian distance between the point and all other points in the set.

functions.name: footballmodels.utils.distance.weighted_l1_distance
documentation:
Calculate the weighted L1 distance between a point and all other points in a set.

Args:
    point (np.ndarray): The point to calculate the distance from.
    all_points (np.ndarray): The set of points to calculate the distance to.
    weights (np.ndarray): The weights to apply to each dimension of the points.

Returns:
    np.ndarray: The weighted L1 distance between the point and all other points in the set.

functions.name: footballmodels.clustering.features.four_factor_model
documentation:
Returns an array of the four factor model features for each event in a dataframe
The factors used are x coordinate of pass origin, y coordinate of pass origin, pass angle, and progressive distance of the pass

Args:
    data (pd.DataFrame): The dataframe.  Must contain columns 'x', 'y', 'endX','endY', and
      'qualifiers', where the qualifiers column is a list of dicts of the form
    {
        "type": {"value": int, "displayName": str},
        "value": int
    }

Returns:
    np.ndarray: An array of the four factor model features for each event in the dataframe

functions.name: footballmodels.opta.dimensions.create_pitch_dims
documentation:
Create pitch dimensions.

Parameters
----------
pitch_type : str
    The pitch type used in the plot.
    The supported pitch types are: 'opta', 'statsbomb', 'tracab',
    'wyscout', 'uefa', 'metricasports', 'custom', 'skillcorner' and 'secondspectrum'.
pitch_length : float, default None
    The pitch length in meters. Only used for the 'tracab' and 'metricasports',
    'skillcorner', 'secondspectrum' and 'custom' pitch_type.
pitch_width : float, default None
    The pitch width in meters. Only used for the 'tracab' and 'metricasports',
    'skillcorner', 'secondspectrum' and 'custom' pitch_type

Returns
-------
dataclass
    A dataclass holding the pitch dimensions.

classes.name: footballmodels.opta.dimensions.BaseDims

methods.name: footballmodels.opta.dimensions.BaseDims.setup_dims
documentation: Run methods for the extra pitch dimensions.

methods.name: footballmodels.opta.dimensions.BaseDims.pitch_markings
documentation:
Create sorted pitch dimensions to enable standardization of coordinates.
and pitch_extent which contains [xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax].

methods.name: footballmodels.opta.dimensions.BaseDims.juego_de_posicion
documentation:
Create Juego de Posición pitch marking dimensions.
See: https://spielverlagerung.com/2014/11/26/juego-de-posicion-a-short-explanation/

methods.name: footballmodels.opta.dimensions.BaseDims.stripes
documentation: Create stripe dimensions.

methods.name: footballmodels.opta.dimensions.BaseDims.penalty_box_dims
documentation:
Create the penalty box dimensions. This is used to calculate the dimensions
inside the penalty boxes for pitches with varying dimensions (width and length varies).
Base dataclass to hold pitch dimensions.

classes.name: footballmodels.opta.dimensions.FixedDims
documentation:
Dataclass holding the dimensions for pitches with fixed dimensions:
'opta', 'wyscout', 'statsbomb' and 'uefa'.

classes.name: footballmodels.opta.dimensions.VariableCenterDims
documentation:
Dataclass holding the dimensions for pitches where the origin is the center of the pitch:
'tracab', 'skillcorner', and 'secondspectrum'.

classes.name: footballmodels.opta.dimensions.CustomDims
documentation:
Dataclass holding the dimension for the custom pitch.
This is a pitch where the dimensions (width/length) vary and the origin is (left, bottom).

classes.name: footballmodels.opta.dimensions.MetricasportsDims
documentation: Dataclass holding the dimensions for the 'metricasports' pitch.

classes.name: footballmodels.opta.dimensions.Standardizer

methods.name: footballmodels.opta.dimensions.Standardizer.transform
documentation:
Transform the coordinates.
Parameters
----------
x, y : array-like or scalar.
    Commonly, these parameters are 1D arrays.
reverse : bool, default False
    If reverse=True then reverse the transform. Therefore, the coordinates
    are converted from pitch_to to pitch_from.
Returns
----------
x_standardized, y_standardized : np.array 1d
    The coordinates standardized in pitch_to coordinates (or pitch_from if reverse=True).

methods.name: footballmodels.opta.dimensions.Standardizer._standardize
documentation: " Helper method to standardize the data

Convert from one set of coordinates to another.
Parameters
\----------
pitch_from, pitch_to : str, default 'statsbomb'

> The pitch to convert the coordinates from (pitch_from) and to (pitch_to).
> The supported pitch types are: 'opta', 'statsbomb', 'tracab',
> 'wyscout', 'uefa', 'metricasports', 'custom', 'skillcorner' and 'secondspectrum'.

length_from, length_to

: The pitch length in meters. Only used for the 'tracab' and 'metricasports',
  'skillcorner', 'secondspectrum' and 'custom' pitch_type.

width_from, width_to

: The pitch width in meters. Only used for the 'tracab' and 'metricasports',
  'skillcorner', 'secondspectrum' and 'custom' pitch_type

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from mplsoccer import Standardizer
>>> standard = Standardizer(pitch_from='statsbomb', pitch_to='custom',                                 length_to=105, width_to=68)
>>> x = [20, 30]
>>> y = [50, 80]
>>> x_std, y_std = standard.transform(x, y)
```

functions.name: footballmodels.opta.actions.open_play_event
documentation:
Determines if an event in the DataFrame represents an open play event.

Parameters:
    df (pd.DataFrame): The DataFrame containing the event data.

Returns:
    pd.Series: A boolean Series indicating whether each event is an open play event.

functions.name: footballmodels.opta.actions.in_attacking_box
documentation:
Determines if the given coordinates in the DataFrame `df` are within the attacking box.

Parameters:
- df (pandas.DataFrame): The DataFrame containing the coordinates.
- start (bool): If True, checks if the coordinates are within the attacking box at the start of the event.
                If False, checks if the coordinates are within the attacking box at the end of the event.

Returns:
- pandas.Series: A boolean Series indicating whether each coordinate is within the attacking box.

functions.name: footballmodels.opta.actions.in_defensive_box
documentation:
Checks if the coordinates in the given DataFrame `df` are within the defensive box.

Parameters:
- df (pandas.DataFrame): The DataFrame containing the coordinates.
- start (bool, optional): If True, checks the starting coordinates (default). If False, checks the ending coordinates.

Returns:
- pandas.Series: A boolean Series indicating whether each coordinate is within the defensive box.

functions.name: footballmodels.opta.actions.is_shot
documentation:
Determines if the given DataFrame `data` contains a shot event.

Parameters:
- data (pandas.DataFrame): The DataFrame containing the event data.

Returns:
- pandas.Series: A boolean Series indicating whether each event is a shot event.

functions.name: footballmodels.opta.actions.xg
documentation:
Calculates the expected goals for each event in the given DataFrame `data`.

Parameters:
- data (pandas.DataFrame): The DataFrame containing the event data.

Returns:
- pandas.Series: A Series containing the expected goals for each event.

functions.name: footballmodels.opta.actions.in_final_third
documentation:
Check if the x-coordinate of each data point is in the final third of the field.

Parameters:
data (pd.DataFrame): The input data containing the x-coordinate.

Returns:
pd.Series: A boolean series indicating whether each data point is in the final third.

functions.name: footballmodels.opta.actions.ppda_qualifying_passes
documentation:
Filters the given DataFrame to select only the qualifying passes for PPDA calculation.

Args:
    data (pd.DataFrame): The DataFrame containing the football event data.

Returns:
    pd.Series: A boolean Series indicating the qualifying passes.

functions.name: footballmodels.opta.actions.open_play_shot
documentation:
Returns a boolean Series indicating whether each row in the input DataFrame represents an open play shot.
An open play shot is defined as a shot that is not from a corner, set piece, penalty, own goal, throw-in set piece, or direct free kick.

Parameters:
- data: pd.DataFrame
    The input DataFrame containing the data.

Returns:
- pd.Series
    A boolean Series indicating whether each row represents an open play shot.

functions.name: footballmodels.opta.actions.set_piece_shot
documentation:
Determines if a given data represents a set piece shot.

Args:
    data (pd.DataFrame): The data to be checked.

Returns:
    pd.Series: True if the data represents a set piece shot, False otherwise.

functions.name: footballmodels.opta.actions.open_play_xg
documentation:
Calculates the expected goals (xG) for open play shots.

Parameters:
    data (pd.DataFrame): The input data containing shot information.

Returns:
    pd.Series: The calculated expected goals for open play shots.

functions.name: footballmodels.opta.actions.open_play_box_goal

Determines if a goal occurred from an open play within the attacking box.

Args:

Args:

functions.name: footballmodels.opta.actions.non_penalty_xg

Calculates the expected goals (xG) for open play shots inside the attacking box.

functions.name: footballmodels.opta.actions.ppda_qualifying_defensive_actions
